title: Sprouts Salad

subs.name: chickpea sprouts or mixed bean sprouts for a higher-protein version
amchur (dry mango powder) — 0.5 tsp gives the same tartness
roasted cumin powder (jeera powder) if chaat masala is unavailable

steps.text: Bring a small saucepan of water to the boil. Add the sprouts and blanch for exactly 2 minutes — they should soften slightly but still retain their crunch. Immediately drain and run under cold water to stop the cooking. This step removes the raw legume smell without cooking away the nutrients.
Drain the sprouts well and pat dry with a clean kitchen cloth. Excess water dilutes the dressing and makes the salad go soggy quickly.
Combine the blanched sprouts, diced tomato, cucumber, onion and green chilli in a mixing bowl.

Add lemon juice, chaat masala and salt. Toss thoroughly so every sprout is coated. Taste and add more lemon or salt if needed — sprouts can absorb seasoning so be a touch more generous than you think necessary.
Top with coriander leaves and serve immediately. For the best texture, eat within 30 minutes of dressing; if meal-prepping, keep the dressing separate and toss just before eating.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: sprouts → chickpea sprouts or mixed bean sprouts for a higher-protein version; lemon → amchur (dry mango powder) — 0.5 tsp gives the same tartness; curry-powder → roasted cumin powder (jeera powder) if chaat masala is unavailable
